etl_bundle_version_notes.1.1.0: LawInstrument, Section, TextChunk include Lex Specialis fields (lex_specialis_v1.json, Phase 3 Step 8).

title: ETL: corpus_v1 chunk row → Neo4j nodes and edges (pilot)
proof_map_rule: Every chunk_id MUST materialize exactly one :TextChunk {chunk_id} so citations always resolve.
idempotency: All loads use MERGE on natural keys (source_doc_id, chunk_id, section_uid). Re-running the same JSONL is safe.
load_order: 1. MERGE LawInstrument by source_doc_id (properties from any chunk of that document).
2. MERGE TextChunk by chunk_id (properties from the row; text optional or externalized).
3. If section_uid is derivable: MERGE Section, then MERGE (Section)-[:PART_OF]->(LawInstrument).
4. MERGE exactly one HAS_CHUNK parent for the TextChunk: Section when present, otherwise LawInstrument.
5. Later stages (3b+): MODIFIES, SUPERSEDES, OVERRIDES, Concept/Relief/RateBand — not emitted by this chunk-only pilot.

section_uid.slug_rules: trim; lowercase; replace runs of non-alphanumeric with single underscore; strip leading/trailing underscores; empty slug → no Section node

TextChunk.text: chunk.text (optional in Neo4j if stored elsewhere)

Section.heading_text: null in pilot (fill in curated ETL later)

always: (Section|LawInstrument)-[:HAS_CHUNK]->(TextChunk) — exactly one parent by pilot rule
